MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Alternatively Summary Adjudication Against Plaintiff James Wasef
Off Calendar Jan-17-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance, or objection, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT OREGA, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively Summary Adjudication, Against Plaintiff James Wasef as follows:

Defendant Orega, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment/adjudication is denied. Orega's motion was filed against plaintiff's original complaint on September 27, 2019. However, a first amended complaint was filed on November 26, with new allegations supported by new discovery responses. There is only one operative complaint in an action, and an amended complaint moots any motion directed at the prior complaint. (State Compensation Ins. Fund v. Sup. Ct. (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1124, 1131.) I note that trial was continued to June 8, 2020 last week.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Alternatively Summary Adjudication Against Plaintiff James Wasef
Off Calendar Nov-19-2019 Continued To Jan-17-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively Summary Adjudication Against Plaintiff James Wasef
Off Calendar Nov-19-2019 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-19-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Gregg Sheffer, Esq. of Sheffer Law, T:415.388.0911, for plaintiff/moving party;
Eric Young, Esq. of Daniel Crowley & Associates, T:415.723.5800, for defendant/opposing party, on Court Call.

RULING: Defendant's counsel requested clarification on the Court's tentative ruling. After hearing, and the parties having stipulated to the Court's conditions, the Court adopted the tentative ruling.

Plaintiff James Wasef's motion for leave to file first amended complaint is granted. Defendant may supplement its pending summary judgment motion to address the new allegations on 30 days' notice. The existing December 30, 2019 hearing on Defendant's motion for summary judgment is continued to January 17, 2020, so that the motions may be heard concurrently. Granting the motion comports with California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings. (See, e.g., Howard v. County of San Diego (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1422, 1428 ["The policy favoring amendment is so strong that it is a rare case in which denial of leave to amend can be justified."].) The conditions on the granting of the motion eliminate any legal prejudice to Defendant by the granting of the motion.

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
